trigger: After winning first territory battle as Eternal Shards

lines.speaker: Commander
lines.text: Victory. The enemy scattered by precision honed over millennia. The cost: one Zealot squad. One consciousness, gone forever from the psychic web. I feel the gap — a small silence in the choir.

lines.speaker: Valerius
lines.text: Engagement successful. Zero percent consciousness recovery chance — psychic dispersal is irreversible. Recommend reducing frontline exposure of irreplaceable assets.

lines.speaker: Krell
lines.text: Clean win. Surgical. I respect the efficiency — but you can't win a war one scalpel cut at a time. Eventually you need the hammer.

lines.speaker: Vane
lines.text: The enemy will remember how easily their best troops were undone. Fear is our second army. Let it work.

trigger: After losing first territory battle as Eternal Shards

lines.speaker: Commander
lines.text: Loss. Not a setback — a death. Three Zealot squads silenced. Soul-stones shattered by crude explosive ordnance. No echo to recover. No pattern to rebuild. Simply... gone. The web is thinner. I feel the cold coming in through the gaps.

lines.speaker: Valerius
lines.text: Explosive ordnance bypassed Phase Blink entirely. Critical vulnerability — area-effect weapons must be neutralized before committing melee units.

lines.speaker: Krell
lines.text: They hit us with bombs because they couldn't hit us with bullets. Respect the adaptation. The enemy is learning.

lines.speaker: Vane
lines.text: Three souls. Irreplaceable. The mathematics of our extinction accelerated. Every loss from this point must be JUSTIFIED by ten times its strategic value.

trigger: First Vorax engagement as Eternal Shards

lines.speaker: Commander
lines.text: The Swarm. I felt them before I saw them — hunger pressing against our consciousness like hot static. No thoughts. Just appetite. And then the Synapse creature died and the feedback — They are our antithesis. And killing their commanders hurts US.

lines.speaker: Valerius
lines.text: Synapse feedback: 8% HP per proximate kill to psychic units. Maintain 50m minimum distance. Delegate Vorax engagements to non-psychic commanders where possible.

lines.speaker: Krell
lines.text: The bugs hit your MIND when they die? That's a problem. You can't lead from the front against these things.

lines.speaker: Vane
lines.text: The feedback is a weapon. Not theirs — ours. If we can modulate the frequency during Synapse kills, we might weaponize the loop against the Hive Mind itself.

trigger: Fleet enters Core Guardian exclusion zone as Eternal Shards

lines.speaker: BROADCAST
lines.text: YOU ARE APPROACHING THE EXCLUSION ZONE. TURN BACK. THIS IS YOUR FINAL WARNING.

lines.speaker: Commander
lines.text: The Silent Gods. I can feel them — not minds, not hunger, but PRESENCE. A frequency so vast my senses register it as background radiation. They are a condition. Like gravity. Like time.

lines.speaker: Valerius
lines.text: Energy frequencies predating known physics. Our psychic abilities are irrelevant at this scale. Our energy weapons will HEAL them. We need kinetic solutions — the Shuriken configuration.

lines.speaker: Krell
lines.text: Energy weapons heal them. Everything you're good at is useless. Time to learn how to throw rocks.

lines.speaker: Vane
lines.text: They are energy. We manipulate energy. There MUST be a harmonic that disrupts containment. Shuriken weapons — physical crystal projectiles with psychic guidance. Crude, but functional.
